metadata.source: Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, China Association of Fragrance Flavor and Cosmetic Industries (CAFFCI), National Bureau of Statistics, 中国香料香精化妆品工业协会

industry_overview.title: China Beauty Industry Overview (2026)
industry_overview.summary: China is the world's second-largest beauty market and the largest producer of cosmetics, skincare, and personal care products. The industry encompasses skincare, color cosmetics, hair care, body care, fragrances, and salon professional products. In 2026, the industry is projected to exceed ¥1.5 trillion in revenue, driven by domestic brand崛起 (rise of local brands), clean beauty trends, male grooming, and cross-border e-commerce growth. The industry is undergoing transformation toward functional efficacy, sustainable packaging, and digital-native brand building.

key_policy_initiatives.name: Action Plan for High-Quality Development of Cosmetics Industry (2024-2026)
key_measures: Strengthen safety supervision and efficacy claims substantiation
Promote innovation in raw materials and formulation technologies
Support development of domestic brands and international expansion
Establish national-level cosmetic testing and evaluation centers
Encourage green manufacturing and sustainable packaging

key_policy_initiatives.name: Regulations on Supervision and Administration of Cosmetics (updated)
key_measures: Mandate efficacy claim substantiation with clinical/test data
Strengthen safety risk monitoring and adverse reaction reporting
Simplify registration process for innovative ingredients

upstream.description: Raw materials, ingredients, packaging, and R&D

subsectors: professional salon products (hair color, perms, treatments)

applications: offline retail (department stores, specialty stores, supermarkets)
cross-border e-commerce (Tmall Global, JD Worldwide, Douyin Global)
export to global markets (Southeast Asia, Japan, Europe, US)

regional_clusters.share_of_industry: 30% of national output (Guangzhou alone accounts for 55% of Guangdong's beauty output) [citation:2]

key_hubs.specialization: Largest beauty manufacturing cluster, 'Guangzhou International Beauty City'
key_hubs.supplier_example: Guangzhou Baiyun Industrial Zone – thousands of manufacturers including Cosmax, Kolmar; also home to Perfect Diary suppliers
Guangzhou Yalian – packaging for L'Oreal, Estee Lauder
Shenzhen Maike Group – ODM for many domestic brands
L'Oreal China R&D, Estee Lauder China, Shanghai Jala (Chando)

key_hubs.specialization: Full-industry chain cluster, '美妆小镇' (Beauty Town)
key_hubs.supplier_example: Proya headquarters and manufacturing base – China's leading skincare brand; over 300 cosmetics companies [citation:4]
Bloomage Biotech – world's largest hyaluronic acid producer, supplies 70% of global market [citation:4]
Yantai Meiqi Cosmetics – OEM for many brands
Fujian Mermaid Hair Products – exports to Africa and Southeast Asia

skincare.description: Largest segment including moisturizers, serums, essences, masks, sunscreens, and eye creams. Dominated by anti-aging, brightening, and moisturizing claims.
skincare.market_trends: Clean beauty; microbiome-friendly; personalized skincare; waterless formulations.

color_cosmetics.description: Lipsticks, foundations, eyeshadows, blushes, mascaras, and nail products. Rapid growth driven by social media and KOL marketing.
color_cosmetics.market_trends: Texture innovation (velvet, water tints); cushion compacts; clean color cosmetics.

hair_care.description: Shampoos, conditioners, treatments, hair masks, styling products, and hair color. Premiumization trend.
hair_care.market_trends: Sulfate-free; bond-building treatments; personalized hair care.

body_care.description: Body lotions, scrubs, soaps, deodorants, and hand creams.
body_care.market_trends: Natural ingredients; sustainable packaging; inclusive fragrances.

fragrances.description: Perfumes, colognes, body mists, and scented products. Emerging category with rapid growth.
fragrances.market_trends: Chinese-inspired scents; unisex fragrances; sustainable sourcing.

supplier_evaluation_metrics.target: ISO 22716 (GMP for cosmetics), ISO 9001; specific certifications (Halal, vegan, cruelty-free)
Compliance with China Cosmetics Supervision Regulations, EU Cosmetic Regulation, FDA requirements
Filling lines (airless, tube, bottle), mixing tanks, QC lab (stability testing, microbial testing)
In-house formulation team, stability testing, efficacy testing capabilities
Sustainable sourcing, clean beauty certifications, plastic reduction initiatives

verification_methods: Request GMP certificate and test reports
Conduct factory audit (cleanroom standards, QC processes)
Order samples for stability testing and sensory evaluation
Use third-party testing for heavy metals, microbial limits, preservative efficacy
communication_best_practices: Provide detailed product specifications (formulation type, claims, target market regulations)
Clarify MOQ and tooling costs for custom molds
Request formulation options or modify existing formulas
Establish quality agreement (AQL levels, acceptable defects)
sourcing_guide.payment_terms: Typical: 30% deposit, 70% balance before shipment. For custom formulations, 50% deposit, 50% on sample approval.

faq.question: What are typical MOQs for beauty products in China?
faq.answer: For stock formulas, MOQ can be 3000-5000 pieces. For custom formulations, MOQ often 5000-10000 pieces. Some contract manufacturers offer lower MOQs (1000-3000) at higher prices. Custom packaging may require 10000-30000 pieces for molds.

faq.question: Do Chinese suppliers provide custom formulation services?
faq.answer: Yes, most contract manufacturers offer ODM services. Provide target claims, texture preferences, and budget. They can develop custom formulas or modify existing ones. NRE fees may apply for complex formulations.

faq.question: How can I ensure my products meet safety and efficacy claims?
faq.answer: Request formulation stability testing, preservative efficacy test, and safety assessment reports. For efficacy claims, clinical testing may be required. Work with third-party labs (SGS, Intertek, TÜV) for verification.

faq.question: What certifications are important for export to different markets?
faq.answer: For EU: CPNP notification, REACH compliance. For US: FDA registration, ingredient review. Halal for Muslim markets. Cruelty-free certifications (Leaping Bunny, PETA). Specify target market requirements early.

faq.question: Can I source clean/clean beauty products in China?
faq.answer: Yes, many suppliers now offer 'clean' formulations (paraben-free, sulfate-free, phthalate-free). Specify your clean criteria. Some have certifications like EWG Verified or COSMOS.

glossary.ODM: Original Design Manufacturer – formulates and produces for client brands.
glossary.OEM: Original Equipment Manufacturer – produces according to client's formula.
glossary.GMP: Good Manufacturing Practice – ISO 22716 for cosmetics.
glossary.NMPA: National Medical Products Administration – China cosmetics regulator.
glossary.Clean beauty: Products free from controversial ingredients (parabens, sulfates, phthalates).
glossary.Sheet mask: Fabric mask soaked in essence, popular in Asia.
glossary.Cushion compact: Liquid foundation in sponge cushion format.
glossary.Halal: Permitted under Islamic law, includes no alcohol or pork derivatives.
